topic: A teacher receives an unexpected gift from a former student
raw_output:
[story 1]

Professor Eleanor Vance was meticulously grading stacks of essays when a small, brightly wrapped box landed silently on her desk. Her hand froze mid-pen stroke. It was a package, tied with a shimmering blue ribbon she didn't recognize. Peeking inside, she saw a collection of vintage vinyl records—music from her undergraduate days. A wave of heat rushed up her neck, making her palms sweat. She quickly covered the box with a sheaf of papers, trying to appear as though she hadn't registered the intrusion. When she finally dared to look again, she saw a small, handwritten note tucked beneath the records: "Thinking of you, El. – Liam." Eleanor’s breath hitched. She felt a sudden, intense urge to hide under her desk, her gaze darting around the quiet classroom as if expecting someone to materialize and demand an explanation.

[story 2]

Mark adjusted his tie for the tenth time, his heart hammering against his ribs like a trapped bird. He hadn't expected anything. Then, a delivery person, looking flustered, dropped a carefully wrapped pastry box onto his mahogany desk. Inside, nestled on tissue paper, was a hand-knitted scarf, the color of deep sapphire. Mark stared at the item, his jaw tightening. He could feel the blood draining from his face, leaving a strange, cold hollowness in his stomach. He glanced toward the door, half-expecting the student to walk in, demanding a conversation. When no one appeared, he picked up the scarf, the yarn feeling impossibly soft and heavy in his hands. He quickly shoved it into a drawer, his fingers trembling slightly as he tried to regain his composure.

[story 3]

Dr. Anya Sharma was halfway through preparing for her evening lecture when she noticed a peculiar item resting on her podium: a small, antique compass, its brass casing tarnished with age. It was clearly not standard classroom material. A sudden, sharp intake of breath escaped her lips. She scanned the room, her eyes sweeping over the empty seats, searching for the source of the offering. A flush crept up her neck, staining her cheeks a vivid crimson. She picked up the compass, turning it over and over in her fingers, the cold metal a stark contrast to the warmth spreading through her chest. She couldn't seem to focus on the lesson plan; every nerve ending felt acutely aware of the unexpected gesture.
